text:
PET-CT SCAN — WHOLE BODY (18F-FDG)

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Margaret Chen, 49-year-old female with a history of acute myeloid leukemia (AML), FLT3-ITD positive, de novo diagnosis, treated with 7+3 induction chemotherapy (cytarabine/daunorubicin) plus midostaurin followed by matched unrelated donor (MUD) allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (10/10 HLA-matched peripheral blood SCT) with fludarabine/busulfan myeloablative conditioning. History of mild chronic graft-versus-host disease (skin and gastrointestinal), managed conservatively. Study performed for 1-year post-transplant restaging and disease surveillance.

PATIENT PREPARATION:
Patient fasted for greater than 6 hours prior to FDG injection. Serum glucose at time of injection: 92 mg/dL (within acceptable range). Weight-based dose of 18F-FDG administered intravenously. Uptake period approximately 60 minutes. Imaging performed from vertex to mid-thigh. Attenuation-corrected and non-attenuation-corrected PET images reviewed alongside low-dose CT in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes. No intravenous contrast administered for CT component. No adverse reaction to the radiopharmaceutical.

COMPARISON:
Prior PET-CT performed at approximately 6 weeks post-diagnosis (pre-transplant restaging): no FDG-avid lesions at that time, complete metabolic response documented. Prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at post-transplant day +90: mild ground-glass opacities right lower lobe noted as resolving at that time.

FINDINGS:

Head and Neck:
No abnormal FDG uptake in the head or neck. No cervical, submandibular, or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy. The parotid and submandibular glands demonstrate symmetric physiologic activity. Thyroid gland appears homogeneous in size and configuration; there is mild diffuse FDG uptake within the thyroid that is slightly above background tissue, which is a nonspecific finding and may be seen in the setting of thyroiditis or hypothyroidism on exogenous thyroid hormone replacement — this is not felt to be pathologically significant in this clinical context. No discrete thyroid nodularity. Oropharyngeal mucosa: no focal areas of hypermetabolism.

Chest:
No mediastinal lymphadenopathy. Prevascular, paratracheal, subcarinal, and hilar nodal stations are all unremarkable in size and demonstrate no abnormal FDG uptake above mediastinal blood pool. Heart demonstrates the expected physiologic FDG uptake in the myocardium. No pericardial effusion. 

Lung parenchyma: The lungs are clear bilaterally. Notably, the mild ground-glass opacities previously noted in the right lower lobe on the day +90 CT have completely resolved and there is no residual opacity, consolidation, or nodularity in this region. No new pulmonary nodules or masses identified on the low-dose CT. No pleural effusions. No pneumothorax. The previously observed small bilateral pleural effusions from the staging scan at diagnosis are no longer present. No endobronchial lesions. No hilar or axillary adenopathy. Chest wall and soft tissues: no FDG-avid lesions.

Upper Abdomen:
Liver: Normal in size, measuring approximately 15.0 cm in craniocaudal dimension (previously 17.1 cm at diagnosis, reduced to 15.3 cm pre-transplant, now fully normalized). The hepatic parenchyma is homogeneous with no focal lesions on CT. No FDG-avid hepatic deposits. There is the expected mild heterogeneous physiologic hepatic background uptake. No biliary ductal dilatation. The gallbladder is unremarkable with no wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid.

Spleen: Normal in size, measuring approximately 10.2 cm in craniocaudal dimension. This represents sustained normalization compared with the splenomegaly of 14.2 cm at the time of initial AML diagnosis and the pre-transplant measurement of 11.8 cm. No FDG-avid splenic lesions. The spleen parenchyma is homogeneous on CT. No accessory spleens identified.

Pancreas: Normal appearance and size. No pancreatic ductal dilatation. No focal pancreatic lesions. No abnormal FDG uptake.

Kidneys and Adrenals: Both kidneys are normal in size with preserved corticomedullary differentiation. There is physiologic excretion of FDG into the collecting systems bilaterally. No solid renal masses. No adrenal nodules or FDG-avid adrenal lesions. Mild physiologic adrenal FDG uptake.

Stomach and Bowel: Diffuse mild physiologic FDG uptake in the gastric wall. Variable physiologic activity throughout the small bowel and colon; no focal areas of hypermetabolism above physiologic bowel activity. No bowel wall thickening to suggest active gastrointestinal GVHD by CT criteria. Given the patient's prior mild GI cGVHD, the bowel appearance is reassuring.

Lower Abdomen and Pelvis:
No pelvic lymphadenopathy. External and internal iliac, obturator, and inguinal nodal stations are all unremarkable. Uterus and ovaries are within normal limits for a premenopausal female, though ovarian activity may be affected by prior conditioning chemotherapy. Urinary bladder demonstrates physiologic FDG excretion. No pelvic free fluid.

Musculoskeletal and Osseous Structures:
The axial and appendicular skeleton is surveyed on the low-dose CT component. No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions. No pathologic fractures. Vertebral body heights are maintained throughout the thoracolumbar spine. Given the patient's exposure to busulfan conditioning and chronic corticosteroid use for GVHD, no gross osseous cortical defects are noted, though bone mineral density assessment is not within the purview of this examination. No FDG-avid lesions in the marrow spaces to suggest discrete focal disease involvement. Diffuse mild-to-moderate FDG uptake throughout the axial skeleton is noted and is consistent with reconstituted marrow activity following allogeneic stem cell transplant — this is a normal and expected finding at this interval post-transplant and does not suggest malignant infiltration.

Soft Tissues:
No FDG-avid soft tissue masses or subcutaneous nodules. No areas of abnormal metabolic activity in the subcutaneous fat or musculature of the trunk or extremities. The skin of the bilateral forearms and dorsal hands — sites of prior lichenoid chronic GVHD — demonstrates no abnormal FDG uptake and no CT-visible skin thickening.

Lymph Nodes (Survey):
No abnormally enlarged or FDG-avid lymph nodes identified in the neck, chest, axillae, mediastinum, abdomen, retroperitoneum, mesentery, or pelvis. Maximum short-axis dimension of visualized lymph nodes does not exceed 8 mm, and none demonstrate uptake above background.

IMPRESSION:

1. No FDG-avid lesions identified anywhere in the surveyed body to suggest active or recurrent acute myeloid leukemia, extramedullary disease, or new malignancy.

2. Complete resolution of the previously noted mild right lower lobe ground-glass opacities compared with the day +90 post-transplant CT scan. The lungs are now clear bilaterally.

3. Continued normalization of spleen and liver size compared with pre-treatment imaging, with spleen now measuring 10.2 cm and liver approximately 15.0 cm — both within normal limits.

4. Diffuse mild axial skeletal marrow FDG uptake is noted, consistent with engrafted donor marrow activity at 12 months post-transplant; this is a physiologic finding and does not suggest disease recurrence.

5. Mild nonspecific diffuse thyroid FDG uptake; clinically correlate with thyroid function tests in the context of known hypothyroidism on levothyroxine therapy.

6. Overall, the imaging findings are consistent with sustained complete metabolic remission at one year following allogeneic stem cell transplantation. No evidence of AML recurrence or extramedullary disease.
